behavior_summary: This script manages a small per-actor 'eggId' state and uses it to gate occasional item spawns when an actor enters a named fast-area trigger. U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea attempts a probabilistic spawn of an item (type 0x018B) chosen from a small range determined by eggId, sets a small random stack size, drops the item at the actor's coordinates, and then marks the actor by adding 0x64 to Egg::eggId. U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea clears or consumes that mark by subtracting 0x64 from Egg::eggId if the stored value is at least 0x64.

The eggId value therefore appears to act as a per-actor staging marker or pending-spawn counter: entry may set/raise the marker after a successful spawn attempt, and exit removes/lowers it to allow future spawns or to record the actor left the area.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea: early skip if Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 OR a random check urandom(0x64) produces a value greater than 0x5A (reduces spawn frequency).

U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea: when not skipped, read eggId into local1, map local1 values (observed 0..6) into a pair of bounds (local3 = low, local2 = high), pick a random item id within that range, create an item, set quantity, pop it at actor coordinates, then Egg::setEggId(Egg::getEggId(this) + 0x64, this).
U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea: if Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 then Egg::setEggId(Egg::getEggId(this) - 0x64, this) — a simple guarded decrement balancing the enter path.
open_questions: What is the semantic meaning of Egg::eggId numeric ranges (how values 0..6 map to concrete spawn categories)? (inferred, confidence: medium)
What exact item type/semantic role corresponds to item type 0x018B? (inferred from usage only, confidence: low)
Is the +0x64 / -0x64 manipulation used strictly as a boolean marker (added once per successful spawn) or as a counter for multiple staged spawns? (confidence: medium)
Is the '0x05h * 0x20h' expression in the raw a constant used for id mapping or a decompiler artifact? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: When an actor leaves the fast-area trigger, remove the per-actor eggId mark by subtracting 0x64 if the stored eggId is at least 0x64. Inferred effect: clear a staged-spawn marker or decrement a pending-spawn counter.
entry_conditions: Invoked on area-exit/U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea event for the actor (fast-area leave trigger).

function_map.name: U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: area-exit handler that decrements/clears Egg::eggId marker

function_map.name: U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea
function_map.role: area-entry handler that probabilistically spawns an item and increments Egg::eggId as a marker

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: read per-actor eggId state used to govern spawning and mapping into spawn ranges

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: write per-actor eggId state; used to add or remove the 0x64 marker

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: instantiate an item instance of a computed id/type and return a handle

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: place/drop the created item into the world at specified coordinates

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: random number generator used for spawn chance and selection

internal_calls.purpose: none observed; U8P_BLANK03::enterFastArea and U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea are complementary area handlers but do not call each other directly in the snippet
none observed; U8P_BLANK03::leaveFastArea is a separate exit handler

external_calls.purpose: read per-actor eggId to decide whether to skip spawning and to select spawn-range index
perform probability check (urandom(0x64) > 0x5A) and select random item id/quantity
create the chosen item instance (id computed from mapped bounds) and return a handle
set a small randomized stack size on the created item
obtain the actor's Z coordinate to pass to Item::pop
obtain the actor's Y coordinate to pass to Item::pop
obtain the actor's X coordinate to pass to Item::pop
drop/place the created item at the actor's position in the world
increment the actor's eggId by 0x64 to mark that a spawn attempt succeeded (or to stage a spawn)
subtract 0x64 from eggId to clear the marker on area exit
